intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments need temporal progression more than tabular snapshots
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data table vs. narrative timeline visualization
facade.label: Timeline strips
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should prioritize chronological storytelling—each experiment shown as a visual thread over time rather than rows and columns
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer structured, comparable data points in grid format where multiple experiments can be scanned side-by-side at a glance

evidence.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments need temporal progression more than tabular snapshots
evidence.implication: Dashboard should prioritize chronological storytelling—each experiment shown as a visual thread over time rather than rows and columns

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer animated fades and slides over instant view changes when navigating between health metrics
facade.label: Smooth transitions between screens
facade.acceptImplies: Favor fluid page transitions, staggered element reveals, and motion-based feedback in navigation flow
facade.rejectImplies: Favor instant loads, no-motion UI, and immediate data visibility without transition delays

facade.hypothesis: A playful, friendly tone with celebratory language and emojis will feel more motivating for daily health tracking than neutral clinical language
facade.label: Cheerful check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: User responds well to warmth and personality; dashboard should use encouraging language, visual celebrations for milestones, conversational UI copy
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clinical precision and minimal tone; dashboard should feel data-focused, clean, restrained—no gamification or emotional language

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers rounded, warm typography and spacious layouts over sharp grids and dense information—suggesting a calm, intimate atmosphere over clinical efficiency.
facade.label: Soft data cards
facade.acceptImplies: Design favors breathing room, rounded corners, and friendly typography; health data should feel approachable, not intimidating.
facade.rejectImplies: User is comfortable with (or prefers) compact, sharp, minimal interfaces; clinical clarity and information density matter more than comfort.

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments prefer high contrast, minimal interfaces that feel clinical and data-focused rather than warm or decorative
facade.axisTargeted: Light vs dark; minimalism vs visual warmth
facade.label: Dark cards, light text
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into stark, high-contrast design with dark backgrounds; de-emphasize illustrations and soft textures
facade.rejectImplies: Explore lighter, friendlier palettes; consider softer visual language and more supportive, less clinical aesthetics

facade.hypothesis: Fixed sidebar navigation reduces cognitive load by keeping experiment categories visible, improving orientation in a personal dashboard
facade.axisTargeted: Layout structure — persistent nav vs collapsible
facade.label: Sidebar always open
facade.acceptImplies: User values constant wayfinding and category visibility; likely prefers stable, always-present reference frame over screen real estate savings
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes screen space and wants nav hidden by default; likely prefers maximizing content area and summoning nav only when needed

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer visual comfort and human-centered data presentation over clinical dashboards when tracking intimate health experiments
facade.axisTargeted: Emotional tone of numerical data (clinical vs. warm vs. playful)
facade.label: Warm metric cards
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should soften numbers with color, whitespace, and friendly typography; data feels less medical, more personal
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should prioritize clean information hierarchy and contrast; warmth in presentation risks obscuring data clarity
